title: Air processing system and method for manufacturing it, headgear having the same and method of processing air
abstract: An air processing system (100, 200, 400, 700, 1200), a headgear (300, 500, 1100A) having such air processing system (100, 200, 400, 700, 1200), a method of processing air, and a method of manufacturing an air processing system (100, 200, 400, 700, 1200) are disclosed. The air processing system (100, 200, 400, 700, 1200) includes a manifold (202, 402, 1102), and air filtration module and an air processing module (1100). The manifold (202, 402, 1102) has at least one air inlet (404, 1104). The air filtration module is disposed in the manifold (202, 402, 1102) and configured to receive an air flow from the at least one air inlet (404, 1104). The air processing module (1100) is disposed in the manifold (202, 402, 1102) and configured to modify at least one property of the air. The manifold (202, 402, 1102) is configured to be mounted to a headgear (300, 500, 1100A), and a weight of the system is less than 1000 g.
claims: Claims 1. An air processing system comprising: a manifold having at least one air inlet; an air filtration module disposed in the manifold and configured to receive an air flow from the at least one air inlet; and an air processing module disposed in the manifold and configured to modify at least one property of the air, wherein the manifold is configured to be mounted to a headgear. 2. The air processing system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the weight of the system is less than 1000g. 3. The air processing system as claimed in claim 2, wherein the weight of the system is less than 750g. 4. The air processing system as claimed in claim 3, wherein the weight of the system is less than 500g. 5. The processing module as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the air processing module is disposed in the manifold downstream of the air filtration module. 6. The air processing system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, further comprising a control unit communicatively coupled to the air processing module and configured to control an operation of the air processing module based on a control signal. 7. The air processing system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the control unit comprises a microphone, and wherein the control signal comprises a signal generated based on a voice command of a wearer of the headgear received by the microphone. 8. The air processing system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the control unit comprises at least one sensor configured to detect at least one property of air on an exterior or interior of the headgear, and wherein the control signal comprises a signal generated by the at least one sensor. 9. The air processing system as claimed in claim 6, wherein the control unit comprises a transceiver configured to be communicatively coupled to a wireless device, and wherein the control signal comprises a signal generated based on data transmitted from the wireless device to the transceiver. 10. The air processing system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the air processing module comprises a temperature management module configured to modify a temperature of the air. 11. The air processing system as claimed in claim 10, wherein the temperature management module comprises at least one thermoelectric module for modifying the temperature of the air. 12. The air processing system as claimed in claim 11 , wherein the at least one thermoelectric module comprises a thermoelectric Peltier chip. 13. The air processing system as claimed in claim 11 or 12, wherein the temperature management module comprises an air channel and a heat sink disposed in the air channel and configured to contact the air flow in the air channel. 14. The air processing system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the air processing module comprises a humidity management module configured to modify a humidity level of the air. 15. The air processing system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the air processing module comprises an odor management module configured to modify an odor of the air. 16. The air processing system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the air processing module comprises a sterilization module configured to substantially sterilize the air. 17. The air processing system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims, wherein the air filtration module comprises a filter, and wherein the air filtration module is configured to provide a signal to indicative of an authenticity of the filter. 18. The air processing system as claimed in claim 17, wherein the air filtration module is configured to provide a signal for replacement of filtration media in the filter. 19. A headgear comprising an impact-resistant shell and the air processing system as claimed in any one of the preceding claims mounted to the shell. 20. The headgear as claimed in claim 19, further comprising a face shield, wherein the face shield is capable of one or more of transmitting visible light, blocking ultra-violet and infra-red radiation, preventing fog formation, resisting scratch and mar, and repelling water. 21. A method of processing air, the method comprising: receiving an air flow through at least one air inlet of a manifold, wherein the manifold is configured to be mounted to a headgear; filtering air in the air flow using an air filtration module disposed in the manifold; and modifying at least one property of the air in the air flow using an air processing module disposed in the manifold, wherein the air processing module is controlled based on a control signal from a control unit communicatively coupled to the air processing module. 22. The method as claimed in claim 21 , wherein the air processing module is disposed in the manifold downstream of the air filtration module. 23. The method as claimed in claim 21 or 22, wherein modifying at least one property of the air in the air flow comprises modifying a temperature of the air. 24. The method as claimed in any one of claims 21 to 23, wherein modifying at least one property of the air in the air flow comprises modifying a humidity level of the air. 25. The method as claimed in any one of claims 21 to 24, wherein modifying at least one property of the air in the air flow comprises modifying an odor of the air. 26. The method as claimed in any one of claims 21 to 25, wherein modifying at least one property of the air in the air flow comprises substantially sterilizing the air. 27. A method of manufacturing an air processing system, the method comprising: providing a manifold having at least one air inlet such that the manifold is mountable to a headgear; disposing an air filtration module in the manifold, wherein the air filtration module is configured to filter air in an air flow received from the at least one air inlet; and disposing an air processing module in the manifold, wherein the air processing module is configured to modify at least one property of the air. 28. The method as claimed in claim 27, wherein the manifold, air filtration module and air processing module are selected such that a weight of the system is less than 000g. 29. The method as claimed in claim 28, wherein the weight of the system is less than 750g. 30. The method as claimed in claim 29, wherein the weight of the system is less than 500g. 31. The method as claimed in any one of claims 27 to 30, wherein disposing the air processing module in the manifold comprises disposing the air processing module downstream of the air filtration module. 32. The method as claimed in any one of claims 27 to 31 , wherein the air processing module comprises a temperature management module configured to modify a temperature of the air. 33. The method as claimed in claim 32, wherein the temperature management module comprises at least one thermoelectric module for modifying the temperature of the air. 34. The method as claimed in claim 33, wherein the at least one thermoelectric module comprises a thermoelectric Peltier chip. 35. The method as claimed in any one of claims 27 to 34, wherein the air filtration module comprises a filter, and wherein the air filtration module is configured to provide a signal to indicative of an authenticity of the filter. 36. The method as claimed in claim 35, wherein the air filtration module is configured to provide a signal for replacement of filtration media in the filter. 37. The air processing system as claimed in any one of claims 1- 8, further comprising an air delivery insert for placement within a headgear. 38. The air processing system as claimed in claim 37, wherein the air delivery insert comprises an inlet connected to the air processing module, and a curved panel connected to the inlet, the curved panel comprising a plurality of apertures for discharging processed air. 39. The air processing system as claimed in claim 38, further comprising an air guide arranged at the end of the curved panel. 40. The air processing system as claimed in claims 11 and 12, further comprising a plurality of thermoelectric modules, each comprising a housing in which one or more thermoelectric Peltier chips are disposed, said housing comprising an inlet for receiving air flow from the atmosphere and an outlet for discharging processed air, and the at least one Peltier chip arranged in a plane having a parallel orientation with respect to the direction of the air flow to contact the air flow. 41. A visor for a headgear comprising the air processing system as claimed in any one of claims 1-18 and 37-40. 42. The visor of claim 41 , wherein the air processing system discharges processed air through apertures in the visor.
